enrichment.short_description: Directory Lister is a free, open-source PHP script that turns any web-accessible folder into a browsable, searchable file listing with zero configuration.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Directory Lister is a single, lightweight PHP application distributed under the MIT license. Dropped into any folder on a PHP-capable web server, it automatically generates a clean, navigable web page listing that folder and all its subdirectories, complete with breadcrumb navigation and download links.

It requires PHP 8.2+ and needs no database or external services, so setup is essentially copy the files, point a web server at the directory, and it works. The project is actively maintained on GitHub under the DirectoryLister organization and has been rewritten multiple times over its long history to modernize the codebase.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: It suits anyone who wants a quick, no-frills way to expose a folder of files for browsing and downloading — homelab operators sharing media or software mirrors, sysadmins publishing internal documentation or build artifacts, and developers who need a fast public file index without standing up a full file-management application.

features.title: Zero-configuration setup
features.description: Copy the script into a directory and it immediately lists all files and subfolders, no database or install wizard required.

features.title: Built-in search
features.description: Users can search for files by name across the listed directory tree.

features.title: README rendering
features.description: Markdown README files found in a directory are rendered inline in the listing.

features.title: File hashes
features.description: Displays file hashes so visitors can verify downloads.

features.title: Light and dark themes
features.description: Includes selectable themes for the listing interface.

features.title: Breadcrumb navigation
features.description: Lets visitors move between parent and child directories easily.

pros: Free, open-source, and MIT-licensed
No database or complex setup — a single script does the job
Fast and lightweight, ideal for static file sharing
Actively maintained with regular releases
cons: Read-only browsing — no built-in file upload or editing
Requires a PHP 8.2+ hosting environment
Not designed as a full multi-user file management system

faqs.question: Is Directory Lister free to use?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free and open source under the MIT license.

faqs.question: What server does it require?
faqs.answer: Any web server that supports PHP 8.2 or newer; no database is needed.

faqs.question: Can I password-protect a listing?
faqs.answer: Access control depends on your web server configuration (e.g. .htaccess); Directory Lister itself focuses on generating the listing.

faqs.question: Does it support downloading files directly?
faqs.answer: Yes, every listed file includes a direct download link.

use_cases.title: Public file sharing
use_cases.description: Publish a folder of downloadable resources, software builds, or documents with a clean browsable interface.

use_cases.title: Internal documentation index
use_cases.description: Expose an internal server directory of docs or reports for a team to browse.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Directory Lister is a free, open-source PHP tool that turns any folder into a browsable, searchable web file listing with zero configuration.

enrichment.short_description: FileGator is a free, open-source, self-hosted multi-user web file manager with drag-and-drop uploads and pluggable storage backends.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: FileGator is an MIT-licensed, self-hosted web file manager built with a PHP backend and a Vue.js frontend. Beyond local disk storage, it can connect to remote storage providers such as Amazon S3, FTP, Dropbox, DigitalOcean Spaces, and Azure Blob Storage through Flysystem storage adapters.

It supports multiple users with distinct roles, permissions, and home folders, and can authenticate against a JSON file, a database, or an existing WordPress installation. Core file operations — copy, move, rename, zip/unzip, preview, upload, and download — are all handled through a single-page web interface, with chunked, resumable drag-and-drop uploads for large files.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: FileGator fits teams and agencies that want a self-hosted alternative to consumer file-sharing tools for internal or client-facing file exchange, developers embedding a file manager into a WordPress site, and anyone who needs one interface to manage files across several storage backends.

features.title: Multi-user access control
features.description: Create multiple users with roles, permissions, and dedicated home folders.

features.title: Pluggable storage adapters
features.description: Store files locally or connect to S3, FTP, Dropbox, DO Spaces, Azure Blob, and other Flysystem-supported backends.

features.title: Multiple auth adapters
features.description: Authenticate users via a JSON file, a database, or an existing WordPress site.

features.title: Chunked drag-and-drop uploads
features.description: Large files upload with progress tracking and pause/resume support.

features.title: Full file operations
features.description: Copy, move, rename, edit, delete, preview, zip, and unzip files from the browser.

features.title: Live demo
features.description: A hosted demo instance lets prospective users try the interface before installing.

pros: Free and open source under MIT license
Works with cloud storage providers, not just local disk
Granular multi-user roles and permissions
WordPress authentication integration for existing sites
cons: Primarily a file manager, not a full digital asset management or backup solution
Setup and customization assume some PHP/Node familiarity
No official managed hosting — self-hosting is required

faqs.question: Is FileGator free?
faqs.answer: Yes, FileGator is free, open-source software under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Can I use cloud storage instead of local disk?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports S3, FTP, Dropbox, Azure Blob, DO Spaces, and other Flysystem adapters.

faqs.question: Can I authenticate users with WordPress?
faqs.answer: Yes, FileGator includes a WordPress auth adapter alongside JSON-file and database options.

faqs.question: Is there a hosted demo?
faqs.answer: Yes, a live demo is available at demo.filegator.io.

use_cases.title: Client file exchange portal
use_cases.description: Give clients a branded, permissioned space to upload and download project files.

use_cases.title: Self-hosted team file hub
use_cases.description: Replace consumer cloud storage apps with a self-hosted file manager connected to your own storage.
use_cases.audience: Small businesses and internal IT teams

enrichment.seo_meta_description: FileGator is a free, open-source, self-hosted multi-user file manager with drag-and-drop uploads and support for S3, FTP, Dropbox, and Azure storage.

enrichment.short_description: ResourceSpace is an open-source digital asset management system for storing, tagging, and sharing media, available self-hosted for free or as a paid managed service.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: ResourceSpace is an open-source Digital Asset Management (DAM) platform developed by Montala Limited, a UK software company founded in 2005. Organizations can download and self-host the software for free, or pay Montala for cloud hosting, on-premise support, and enterprise services.

The platform is built to store, organize, search, and share large volumes of digital media, with features such as metadata-driven search, granular permission controls, version management, usage reporting, and audit trails aimed at organizations that manage sensitive or regulated content.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: ResourceSpace is commonly used by charities, museums and cultural institutions, media companies, public-sector bodies, and educational institutions that need a controlled, searchable repository for images, video, and documents, whether run on their own infrastructure or through Montala's managed cloud plans.

features.title: Metadata-driven search
features.description: Find assets quickly using structured metadata fields and full-text search.

features.title: Granular permissions
features.description: Control access to collections and individual assets by user, group, or role.

features.title: Version management
features.description: Track and manage multiple versions of the same asset over time.

features.title: API access
features.description: Integrate ResourceSpace with other systems using its API.

features.title: Audit trails
features.description: Log usage and downloads for compliance and reporting purposes.

features.title: Self-hosted or managed cloud
features.description: Run the open-source software on your own servers or subscribe to Montala's hosted and supported plans.

pros: Core software is free and open source
Long-established project (since 2005) with an active user base in the cultural and nonprofit sectors
Strong permissioning and audit features for regulated content
Optional managed hosting and support for organizations that don't want to self-host
cons: Self-hosting requires server administration knowledge
Managed cloud and enterprise plans carry significant monthly costs
Interface and setup can feel dated compared to newer commercial DAM tools

faqs.question: Is ResourceSpace free?
faqs.answer: The core software is free and open source and can be self-hosted at no licensing cost. Montala also sells paid cloud hosting, on-premise support, and enterprise plans.

faqs.question: Who maintains ResourceSpace?
faqs.answer: It is developed by Montala Limited, a UK-based software company.

faqs.question: Does it support single sign-on?
faqs.answer: Yes, SSO integration is available.

faqs.question: Is there an API?
faqs.answer: Yes, ResourceSpace provides an API for custom integrations.

use_cases.title: Cultural and museum archives
use_cases.description: Store and make searchable large collections of historical images and documents.

use_cases.title: Brand and marketing asset libraries
use_cases.description: Give teams a permissioned, searchable repository for approved marketing media.

use_cases.title: Regulated document management
use_cases.description: Track versions and maintain audit trails for sensitive organizational content.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: ResourceSpace is an open-source digital asset management system for storing, tagging, and sharing media, free to self-host or available as a paid cloud service.

name: slcl
skip_reason: slcl is an extremely obscure single-maintainer project hosted on a private Gitea instance (gitea.privatedns.org) with no accessible README, license file, installation docs, or landing page beyond a one-line repository description ('small and lightweight cloud storage written in C99 and POSIX.1-2008'). There is insufficient verifiable public information to write a factual, non-fabricated product page.

enrichment.short_description: Surfer is a free, open-source static file server with a web interface, CLI, WebDAV, and SFTP access, built for self-hosting on Cloudron.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Surfer is an open-source (MIT-licensed) static file server, developed by the Cloudron team and packaged for self-hosting on the Cloudron platform. It serves static websites and files, and lets you manage content four different ways: a web-based file manager at /_admin, a command-line tool (installable via npm), a WebDAV endpoint you can mount as a network drive, and SFTP access for clients like FileZilla.

It automatically serves index.html/index.htm files, supports custom 404 pages, and offers three access levels for a site — fully public, password-protected, or restricted to logged-in Cloudron users — making it a lightweight option for hosting static sites or build output from static site generators.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Surfer suits people already self-hosting apps on Cloudron who need a simple place to host static websites, documentation sites, or build artifacts from a static site generator, and developers who want to script uploads via its CLI as part of a CI/CD pipeline.

features.title: Web-based file manager
features.description: Upload and manage files and folders directly from a browser at /_admin.

features.title: CLI tool
features.description: The cloudron-surfer npm package lets you upload files from the command line using an API token, useful for automated deploys.

features.title: WebDAV support
features.description: Mount your Surfer site as a network drive on Windows, macOS, or Linux.

features.title: SFTP access
features.description: Upload files with any standard SFTP client such as FileZilla.

features.title: Configurable access control
features.description: Serve a site as fully public, password-protected, or restricted to Cloudron users.

features.title: Custom index and error pages
features.description: Set a custom index file and a custom 404 error page.

pros: Free and open source under the MIT license
Multiple upload methods (web, CLI, WebDAV, SFTP) for different workflows
Good fit for CI/CD static site deployment via the CLI
Actively documented as part of the Cloudron app catalog
cons: Designed to run on the Cloudron platform rather than as a fully standalone install
Static file hosting only — no server-side application logic
Smaller community than general-purpose static hosting tools

faqs.question: Is Surfer free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free, open-source software under the MIT license.

faqs.question: Do I need Cloudron to use Surfer?
faqs.answer: Surfer is packaged and documented as a Cloudron app; the source is also mirrored on GitHub for those running Cloudron-style self-hosting.

faqs.question: Can I automate deployments?
faqs.answer: Yes, the CLI tool supports scripted uploads using an API access token, suitable for CI/CD pipelines.

faqs.question: Can I password-protect a site?
faqs.answer: Yes, sites can be set to public, password-protected, or Cloudron-user-only access.

use_cases.title: Static site hosting
use_cases.description: Host output from static site generators (Jekyll, Hugo, etc.) with simple drag-and-drop or CLI uploads.
use_cases.audience: Developers and site owners on Cloudron

use_cases.title: CI/CD static deploys
use_cases.description: Push build artifacts automatically to Surfer as part of a deployment pipeline using the CLI and API tokens.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Surfer is a free, open-source static file server for Cloudron with a web interface, CLI, WebDAV, and SFTP upload options.

enrichment.short_description: TagSpaces is an offline-first file organizer that lets you tag, note, and browse files directly on disk without cloud accounts, with a free desktop app and paid Pro tiers.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: TagSpaces is a file-based organization tool: instead of importing files into a database, it lets you add color-coded tags, notes, and metadata directly to files and folders on your own disk or cloud-synced storage. The core desktop app, TagSpaces Lite, is free and open source under the AGPL license and runs offline with no account required.

TagSpaces Pro adds collaborative tagging, AI-assisted tag generation and summarization (run offline), advanced search, Kanban boards, and calendar/map perspectives, sold as an annual per-user subscription or a one-time license. A Custom/enterprise tier adds on-premises deployment and white-labeling for organizations.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: It fits people who want to organize large personal or professional file collections (documents, photos, research) without locking them into a proprietary cloud database, plus teams that want shared tagging and richer views (Pro) while keeping files on their own storage.

features.title: File-based tagging
features.description: Add color-coded tags directly to file and folder names, no external database required.

features.title: Notes and Markdown editing
features.description: Create and edit notes in Markdown, HTML, or plain text alongside your files.

features.title: 50+ file format previews
features.description: Preview a wide range of file types without leaving the app.

features.title: Offline AI tagging and summarization
features.description: TagSpaces Pro can generate tags and summaries using AI models that run offline.

features.title: Kanban and map/calendar perspectives
features.description: View and organize files as Kanban boards, on a map, or on a calendar (Pro).

features.title: Web clipper
features.description: A browser extension for Chrome, Firefox, and Edge to save web content into your TagSpaces workspace.

pros: Files stay in their native location — no vendor lock-in or proprietary database
Free, open-source Lite version covers core tagging and organization
Works fully offline across desktop, mobile, and web (Pro)
Self-funded and independent, without external investors
cons: Advanced features (AI tagging, Kanban, collaboration) require a paid Pro subscription
Web and enterprise/on-premises deployment require the paid Custom tier
Tag-in-filename approach can create long filenames on some systems

faqs.question: Is TagSpaces free?
faqs.answer: TagSpaces Lite, the core desktop app, is free and open source under the AGPL license. TagSpaces Pro and Custom are paid tiers with additional features.

faqs.question: What platforms does it support?
faqs.answer: Desktop apps for Windows, macOS, and Linux; mobile apps for iOS and Android; and a web version and browser extension through the Pro and Custom tiers.

faqs.question: Does it require the cloud?
faqs.answer: No, TagSpaces works fully offline; it can optionally connect to S3-compatible cloud storage.

faqs.question: Can businesses self-host it?
faqs.answer: The Custom/enterprise tier supports on-premises deployment and white-labeling.

use_cases.title: Personal file organization
use_cases.description: Tag and organize documents, photos, and research files stored locally or in synced cloud folders.

use_cases.title: Team knowledge and document tagging
use_cases.description: Use collaborative tags and Kanban/calendar views to organize shared file repositories.

pricing_plans.price: Paid per-user subscription (annual) or one-time license
features: Offline AI tag generation and summarization

enrichment.seo_meta_description: TagSpaces is an offline-first file organizer for tagging and browsing files on your own disk, with a free open-source app and paid Pro tiers.

enrichment.short_description: Tiny File Manager is a free, open-source, single-file PHP web file manager with a built-in code editor and no database requirement.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Tiny File Manager is a self-hosted web file manager distributed as a single PHP file under the GNU GPL v3.0 license. It requires PHP 5.5 or higher, no database, and can be dropped onto any Apache or Nginx server (or run via the provided Docker image) to get a full browser-based file management interface.

It supports uploading, downloading, editing, previewing, and organizing files, including zip/unzip, copy/move between folders, and a built-in code editor with syntax highlighting for over 150 languages. Multi-user access with configurable permission levels is supported, along with CSRF protection and secure password hashing.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: It's popular with developers and hosting administrators who need a lightweight, drop-in file manager for a shared or VPS hosting environment where installing a full application stack isn't practical.

features.title: Single-file install
features.description: The entire application ships as one PHP file with no database dependency.

features.title: Built-in code editor
features.description: Edit files directly in the browser with syntax highlighting for 150+ languages.

features.title: File previews
features.description: Preview images, video, audio, PDFs, and text files without downloading them.

features.title: Multi-user support
features.description: Configure multiple users with different access levels.

features.title: Zip and batch operations
features.description: Create zip archives and perform batch copy, move, or delete operations.

features.title: Docker support
features.description: Official Docker image available for containerized deployment.

pros: Free and open source under GPL v3.0
Extremely easy to deploy — a single file, no database
Built-in code editor useful for quick server-side edits
Available in 50+ languages
cons: Single-file design has drawn scrutiny for the need to keep it updated against security issues, since it's a common target when misconfigured on public servers
Feature set is more basic than dedicated DAM or sync tools
Best suited for smaller deployments rather than large multi-team environments

faqs.question: Is Tiny File Manager free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free and open source under the GNU GPL v3.0 license.

faqs.question: Does it need a database?
faqs.answer: No, it runs from a single PHP file with no database required.

faqs.question: Can multiple users share it?
faqs.answer: Yes, it supports multiple users with configurable access levels.

faqs.question: Can I run it in Docker?
faqs.answer: Yes, an official Docker image is available.

use_cases.title: Quick server file management
use_cases.description: Drop a single PHP file onto a shared or VPS server to get instant browser-based file management.

use_cases.title: In-browser code editing
use_cases.description: Edit configuration files or scripts directly on the server without SSH access.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Tiny File Manager is a free, open-source, single-file PHP file manager with a built-in code editor and no database requirement.

enrichment.short_description: 0 A.D. is a free, open-source real-time strategy game of ancient warfare, playable offline, on LAN, or online multiplayer.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: 0 A.D. is a real-time strategy game developed by Wildfire Games, released as free and open-source software: the code is licensed under GPL v2 and the artwork under CC-BY-SA 3.0. There is no freemium model, in-game advertising, or paid DLC — the full game is free to download and play on Windows, macOS, and Linux.

Players build and manage ancient civilizations, gathering resources, training units, and waging tactical battles across historical settings. The game includes 15 playable civilizations such as the Romans, Carthaginians, Persians, and Han Dynasty, and supports both single-player campaigns/skirmishes and online multiplayer through a matchmaking lobby or direct LAN/IP connections that players can host themselves.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: 0 A.D. appeals to real-time strategy fans who want a deep, historically themed RTS without any cost, and to homelab/LAN party organizers who want a game that can be hosted directly between players without a commercial matchmaking service.

features.title: 15 playable civilizations
features.description: Command historically themed factions including Romans, Carthaginians, Persians, and the Han Dynasty.

features.title: Multiplayer lobby and LAN play
features.description: Host or join matches via an online lobby, direct IP connection, or local network.

features.title: Customizable match setup
features.description: Configure player counts, teams, and population limits before a game.

features.title: Historically detailed units and buildings
features.description: Includes period-appropriate technology trees, buildings, and gendered civilian unit models.

features.title: Cross-platform releases
features.description: Available for Windows, macOS, and Linux, including AppImage, Snap, and Flatpak builds.

features.title: No monetization
features.description: No freemium mechanics, ads, or paid content of any kind.

pros: Completely free with no ads or microtransactions
Open-source code and artwork under permissive licenses
Deep RTS mechanics with 15 distinct civilizations
Player-hostable multiplayer via LAN or direct IP
cons: Still under active alpha/beta-style development with occasional rough edges
No official mobile release
Smaller multiplayer population than major commercial RTS titles

faqs.question: Is 0 A.D. really free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is completely free with no freemium model, ads, or paid content.

faqs.question: What platforms does it run on?
faqs.answer: Windows 10/11, macOS 10.15+, and Linux, with several Linux packaging formats available.

faqs.question: Can I host my own multiplayer matches?
faqs.answer: Yes, players can host games directly over LAN or a direct IP connection, in addition to the public matchmaking lobby.

faqs.question: Is the source code available?
faqs.answer: Yes, the game code is licensed under GPL v2 and the artwork under CC-BY-SA 3.0.

use_cases.title: Free RTS gaming
use_cases.description: Play a full-featured real-time strategy game with no cost or ads.

use_cases.title: LAN party or self-hosted matches
use_cases.description: Host multiplayer matches directly between players without relying on a commercial server.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: 0 A.D. is a free, open-source real-time strategy game of ancient warfare for Windows, macOS, and Linux, with LAN and online multiplayer.

enrichment.short_description: DDraceNetwork (DDNet) is a free, open-source cooperative multiplayer platformer mod of Teeworlds, playable with up to 64 players per map.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: DDraceNetwork (DDNet) is a free, open-source, community-maintained modification of DDRace, itself built on the Teeworlds engine. Its source code is publicly hosted on GitHub. Instead of player-versus-player combat, DDNet's core gameplay has players cooperate to navigate custom, community-made maps together, with up to 64 players able to join a single map.

The game includes a global ranking system, official international tournaments, in-game map-making tools, and both official worldwide servers and support for privately hosted servers. It's available as a direct download for Windows, macOS, and Linux, and has also been distributed on Steam since 2020.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: DDNet suits players who enjoy cooperative precision-platforming challenges and community-made content, as well as server operators and communities who want to run their own private DDNet servers for custom maps or events.

features.title: Cooperative platforming
features.description: Up to 64 players can work together to complete custom maps.

features.title: Global ranking system
features.description: Tracks points and rankings across official servers and tournaments.

features.title: International tournaments
features.description: Organized competitive events where players compete for rankings.

features.title: Custom map support
features.description: Community-created maps and in-game map-making tools.

features.title: Private server hosting
features.description: Communities can run their own DDNet servers alongside the official worldwide servers.

features.title: Cross-platform availability
features.description: Direct downloads for Windows, macOS, and Linux, plus a Steam release.

pros: Completely free to play with open-source code
Large library of community-made cooperative maps
Active tournament and ranking scene
Communities can self-host private servers
cons: Niche genre (cooperative precision platformer) with a learning curve
Retro pixel-art presentation may not appeal to all players
Relies on voluntary donations to fund official server infrastructure

faqs.question: Is DDraceNetwork free?
faqs.answer: Yes, it is free to play and open source, with optional voluntary donations to support server costs.

faqs.question: Can I host my own server?
faqs.answer: Yes, DDNet supports privately hosted servers alongside the official worldwide server network.

faqs.question: What platforms are supported?
faqs.answer: Windows, macOS, and Linux, plus a Steam release.

faqs.question: How many players can play together?
faqs.answer: Up to 64 players can join a single cooperative map.

use_cases.title: Cooperative multiplayer platforming
use_cases.description: Team up with other players to complete community-made maps.

use_cases.title: Community server hosting
use_cases.description: Run a private DDNet server for a gaming community or friend group.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: DDraceNetwork (DDNet) is a free, open-source cooperative multiplayer platformer mod of Teeworlds, supporting up to 64 players and private server hosting.

enrichment.short_description: Digibuzzer is a free, open-source, self-hostable web app by La Digitale for running live buzzer-style quiz games in a virtual game room.

long_form_blocks.heading: Overview
paragraphs: Digibuzzer is a free web application from La Digitale, a French developer of open-source educational tools. It lets a host run a live quiz-style activity where participants join a virtual game room from their own phone or computer and race to be the first to answer, similar in spirit to a TV quiz buzzer.

The hosted version at digibuzzer.app requires no account or registration and collects no participant data. The project's source code is published on Codeberg under the AGPL-3.0 license, and it can also be self-hosted using Node.js and Redis, or deployed with the provided Docker configuration, for organizations that want to run their own instance.

long_form_blocks.heading: Who It's For
paragraphs: Digibuzzer is built primarily for teachers and trainers running live review sessions or quizzes, in person or remotely, and for anyone who wants a simple, self-hostable, account-free buzzer game for interactive sessions.

features.title: Virtual buzzer game rooms
features.description: Host a live quiz where participants compete to answer first from their own device.

features.title: No account required
features.description: Both hosts and participants can join without registering or providing personal data.

features.title: Host-controlled scoring
features.description: The host validates or rejects answers and points/rankings update automatically.

features.title: Self-hostable
features.description: Open-source under AGPL-3.0, deployable via Node.js/Redis or Docker for organizations that want their own instance.

features.title: Multi-language interface
features.description: Available in multiple languages including French, English, Italian, German, and Spanish.

pros: Free and open source under AGPL-3.0
No account or data collection required to use the hosted version
Simple, fast setup for live classroom or training activities
Self-hostable via Docker for organizations with data or privacy requirements
cons: Feature set is narrowly focused on the buzzer quiz format rather than general quiz-building
Self-hosting requires Node.js and Redis knowledge
Documentation is primarily oriented toward French-speaking educators

faqs.question: Is Digibuzzer free?
faqs.answer: Yes, the hosted version at digibuzzer.app is free, and the source code is open source under AGPL-3.0.

faqs.question: Do participants need an account?
faqs.answer: No, both the host and participants can join a game room without registering.

faqs.question: Can I self-host Digibuzzer?
faqs.answer: Yes, it can be deployed with Node.js and Redis or via the provided Docker configuration.

faqs.question: Who makes Digibuzzer?
faqs.answer: It's developed by La Digitale, a French team building open-source educational web tools.

use_cases.title: Classroom review games
use_cases.description: Run a live buzzer-style question and answer session to review material with students.

use_cases.title: Remote or in-person team quizzes
use_cases.description: Host a competitive quiz activity for a group, in person or over video call.

enrichment.seo_meta_description: Digibuzzer is a free, open-source, self-hostable web app for running live buzzer-style quiz games with no account or data collection required.
